---
title: "Dygraphs"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Time series

```{r}
library(xts) #eXtensible time series data
library(dygraphs)
```

## From 2020

```{r}
by_date <- detentions |>
  filter(!is.na(`Stay Book In Date Time`)) |>
  group_by(`Stay Book In Date Time`) |>
  dplyr::summarize(count = n())
```

## Transform the new data frame into a type suitable for time series analysis.

```{r}
by_date <- xts(by_date$count, order.by = by_date$`Stay Book In Date Time`)
```

## Create chart using dygraph packages of interactive time series charts

```{r}
dygraph(by_date, 
        main = "<font size=5>ICE Detentions 2020 to Oct 2025</font> <br> <small> deportationdata.org </small>",
        ylab = "Frequency") |>
  dyRangeSelector() |>
  dySeries("V1", label = "Frequency", color = "#6b705c") |>
  dyLegend(show = "follow") |>
  dyOptions( fillGraph = TRUE)
```
